hypothesis: Early capture moves the initial degraded side fall out of the inverted basin. The near-feasible candidate lost posture because it replaced the gradual rise too early, while the first candidate reached full height but kept injecting roll/pitch feedback after alpha reached one. Preserving the complete rise and disabling only that feedback at the final stand should keep support while removing the limit cycle.
expectedEffect: Qualify or materially stabilize impact-left-degraded, preserve the baseline right-degraded retry and every exact/static path, and remove the remaining recovery-induced transition regression.
